sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: The Raven Chaser
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Detective Conan: The Raven Chaser for the same reason it broke through in 2009: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Yasuichiro Yamamoto keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Murder, My Sweet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Raven Chaser clicked for you, Murder, My Sweet (1944) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Raven Chaser clicked for you, Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and crime, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: The Raven Chaser?
faq.answer: Try Murder, My Sweet, Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target, Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: The Raven Chaser while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: The Raven Chaser?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: The Raven Chaser (2009) is best described as Animation, Crime, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: The Raven Chaser part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
